Let's go back to the plot. It's you again, we're all going through step by step how to create a compelling vision for your future. When you're writing out the vision, number one, you have to think big. You have to go after and say, you know, on the highest level without being held down by my limitations that I'm only mediocre, which is not true. You, everyone, I don't care who you are, you're capable of greatness. It's just a matter of working to learn the skill sets that you need to learn. That's what you're doing now. But if you go into with that mindset that I am capable of anything, then you will write out a vision that truly is inspiring and that when you read it back to yourself, you're like, that is something that I could, it's worth, you know, really going after because here's what happens. I will tell you this, bottom line. I'll tell you too, there's an awful lot of hard work that stands between where you are right now and where your vision is. It ain't going to be easy. There's going to be work involved. So if you're not totally turned on and when you think about that vision, if it's not like, oh boy, you know, it just, it seems like it's so worth it. Guess what? Your brain will say, eh, not worth all the work. So that's what happens. See some people, when they think about what the actual end result is, the prize, so to speak, at the end of the day, and the prize ain't so great, then their brain, smart as it is, will say, why do all the work? If the payoff is not that great, why do all the work? And conversely, if you actually are in a situation where your vision is so inspiring and the payoff is so dynamic and so fruitful and so empowering to that when you actually think about it and you read it, these are like, it's worth it. The will do the work. You'll go through the batteries, you'll go through the obstacles. This is going to be obstacles. It's going to be setbacks along the way. It's never straight line to success. We wish it was, but it's just not. It's going to be all these detours and problems and obstacles that come in that just kind of mess you up. And if you have that amazing vision, though, that truly inspires, you'll say, it's worth it. I'll do the work. I'll overcome the obstacles. So that's the first thing that really, you have to drop the negative voices, the nay-saying voices of the past and write a compelling vision that is thinking big. That's about the best you can be on the high level. That's number one. The second thing that you have to do is you have to write it in the actual present tense. In five years, I see myself doing, not like in three years now, maybe I'll have this, maybe I'll have this. It's got to be, I am doing this. I am living here. I am driving this car. My wife is doing this. My children are attending these schools. My parents now are no longer struggling day to day. I am moving up now. I am working each day and feeling great. I see, and you start speaking in the present tense and your brain starts to make it real. So you want to project three to five years down the road, think big and bold, unlimited possibility. And by the way, of course, in five years, I want to be working in Jupiter and be the king of, I would like the US to give me the territory of Jupiter as mine. I'm going to be living on one of the titans and I'll be ruling. That's your vision. And that's not reality. So it has to be, I'm not saying make some craziness up. I'm talking about keep it grounded in reality, but go to that highest level of reality. There is a balancing point. So I don't need that. That's just delusional, right? Yeah, it becomes delusion. Your brain is too smart for that. And what happens when you're lying in bed and your brain is like, we know, you know that I know. You know, you say it's really all crap, right? So it's got to essentially be grounded in reality, but at that high level, unlimited possibility. One of my, let's say one of my favorite businessmen, do I really love and respect this? James Cameron. He's a director of Titanic and Avatar. And I say he's a businessman because besides being, you know, an unbelievable director and an artist, he's also a businessman. The reason why is I heard an interview that he did maybe a year or two ago when he, Avatar came out and they were like, you know, how did you stick with this avatar for 10 years and you dunk your own money into it? And you know, you just put everything on the line and you know, and you know, how is it that you have now two, the two biggest grossing movies of all time? What's the chance that one director would have $2 billion? It just seems outrageously impossible, right? He goes, well, I'll tell you what I do. And this is without even knowing anything about the intergame. Just naturally he revealed it. He goes, here's what I do. I think really, really, really, really big. This way, if I'm only half right, I'm still doing great. That's what James Cameron said. And herein lies one of the great secrets to revision is that it has to be so amazing that even if only half it comes true, like, damn, I'm doing great. If your vision is only going to be great, if everything happens, typically it won't work out that way. And your vision is also malleable. It's constantly changing. It's updating. I'm just feeling so great and I'm living my vision like right now, like I'm living my own vision. I don't have to look at it each day because it's just it's me. I'm congruent. My life force is where is on my vision path. It's when I feel like I'm off path that I make sure that every single day, sometimes twice, even three times a day, I'll put it on my screensaver, on my phone. So every time I look at the phone, it's in my face, what I got to be doing, where I'm going in life to remind myself, to train myself not to get caught up in all the daily noise because that's the danger. The danger is that you have this unbelievably compelling vision, but along the way, there's the daily grind. And typically what happens is we get so caught up in our so-called problems that we end up focusing on the problem, living in the problem, so to speak, versus living and essentially going towards and focusing on the solution. To develop something so detailed, obviously entails quite a bit of brain power, a bit of creative thinking. I know you have that ability. What advice would you give to someone who's struggling trying to create that vision, just doesn't know where to start, doesn't know where to see themselves? I don't think it takes much brain power past maybe a seventh or eighth grade education. I don't think it's brain power. It's not that. It's commitment to actually sitting down and doing it. If you can write English or whatever language you speak, okay, and you are smart enough to have gotten a job and you're, I'm doing this into you for this training program. If you're sitting watching this right now, you're smart enough to do a vision statement. So that's not what's holding you back. If you're being held back right now from writing your vision statement, you're simply just not trying. You have the skill sets to do it. So what I would do in that case, let's say you're, you know, you're, you're blocked. You just can't, for some reason, you can't get off the mark. You can't get past square one. I would start in a different way. I'd say rather than my vision, let's start off with my why first. Why is success important to me? Why do I really want to achieve? And you know, just to say, let's call your vision achievement. Why do I want to have achievement in my life? Why does it matter? Who, what are the things in my life that really are important that are really my drivers, right? At the deepest level, we always move away from things that cause us pain and we gravitate towards things that give us pleasure. So I would say what in your life, in your life gives you ultimate pleasure. What does ultimate pleasure mean to you? And what does ultimate pain mean to you? It's very different for me than it is to you. Everyone's different. We all have our own beliefs, our own experiences, our own values. And based on those beliefs, values and experiences, we develop ideas of what means ultimate pleasure to us.
